{{Quote|Although the Clone Wars were over, some people never seem to get the message. The worst case of denial was Gizor Delso, a Geonosian Separatist who'd somehow escaped Lord Vader's efforts to wipe out the remnants of the CIS. Lord Vader tracked him to Mustafar, and brought us in to finish the job.|[[Retired clone trooper]]|audio=GizorDellso1.flac|<ref name="BFII" />}}
The '''Battle of Mustafar''' was an isolated battle on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] that took place between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]] in the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of Imperial rule. The engagement was fought by the Imperial [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] under the command of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], against a [[Gizor Dellso's army|secret droid army]] controlled by [[Gizor Dellso]]. The battle resulted in the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Dellso and the destruction of his Separatist forces.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|By the time we'd made our way to the molten surface of Mustafar, it was apparent that Dellso had been a busy little bug. All by his lonesome self he'd found a previously hidden droid factory. Fired it up and cranked out his own private army of battle droids. Needless to say this got the men of the 501st a little hot under the helmet, and not just because of all that lava.|Retired clone trooper|<ref name="BFII" />}}
A [[Geonosian/Legends|Geonosian]] engineer, Gizor Dellso was a committed and loyal member of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|CIS]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. After the [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|assassination]] of the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist Council]] at the end of the war by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], Dellso refused to surrender. In possession of stolen [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] starfighter plans, fled to [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]]<ref name="db">{{DB|character|gizordellso|Dellso, Gizor}}</ref> in the early days of the
Empire.<ref name="SWGTCGPOGG">[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Galaxies: The Complete Guide'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref>

[[File:DroidSchematics.jpg|thumb|left|160px|The [[Confederate infantry prototype]].]]

Gizor Dellso was one of the engineers who constructed the signal override apparatus of the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]]. He was therefore able fire up an entire [[Techno Union droid factory/Legends|droid factory]] sitting dormant on Mustafar, and in the short time it took the Empire to find him he had reactivated his own [[Gizor Dellso's army|droid army]] and [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], which did not respond to Darth Vader's deactivation of the droids.<ref name="db" /> Dellso also planned to use the hidden facility on the planet to create a new type of [[Confederate infantry prototype|superior battle droid]].<ref name="BFII" />

==The battle==
===Space battle===
{{Quote|It seemed like a simple enough mission, but when we saw the fleet of droid ships, hovering over the planet, we suddenly realized that Delso's denial mechanisms were more dangerous than we'd imagined|Retired clone trooper|audio=GizorDellso3.flac|<ref name="BFII" />}}
[[File:BF_Mustafar_Hangar.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Separatist battle droids assault the Imperial Star Destroyer.]]
The members of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], were transported to Mustafar by an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] to destroy Gizor Dellso's uprising. However, the ship's hangar was immediately invaded by one of the [[HMP droid gunship/Legends|HMPs]] from [[Gizor Dellso's navy]], which dispatched [[OOM pilot battle droid/Legends|OOM pilot battle droids]] and [[Droid marine]]s onto the ship. Although one of the pilots panicked when the Empire failed to launch their starfighters, the Imperial Officer sent troops out, which held the droids back. To destroy the gunship, [[Imperial Marines/Legends|Imperial Marines]] used their rocket launchers and thermal detonators, while the [[TIE pilot]]s deployed time bombs. After the gunship was destroyed, the marines and pilots then used their blasters to eliminate the remaining invaders and manned their starfighters.<ref name="BFII" />

After destroying a small number of Dellso's fighters, the pilots successfully knocked out the shields on the [[Providence-class carrier/destroyer/Legends|''Providence''-class destroyer]]. Then, at Darth Vader's unusual request, a pilot flew into the enemy hangar and stole one [[Belbullab-24 strike bomber]]. The fighter was then brought to the Imperial hangar for closer study.<ref name="BFII" /> The vulnerable droid vessel was then pounded by turbolaser blasts from the nearby [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], inflicting massive damage to all critical systems including the command deck.

Dellso's space blockade had been demolished. However, his ground forces, waiting in the [[Klegger Corp Mining Facility/Legends|Klegger Corp Mining Facility]], were ready for attack.<ref name="BFII" />

===Siege of the mining facility===
{{Quote|Any droid we don't destroy today will come back to haunt us tomorrow.|Unidentified Imperial officer, during the battle|<ref name="BFII" />}}
[[File:BF_Mustafar_2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The 501st storms the facility.]]
The troopers of the 501st Legion were deployed on a landing platform outside the mining facility on Mustafar. Many had just stepped foot on the platform when droids shot them dead from the open windows of the entrance corridor. As [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] scurried out onto the landing platform, the 501st was able to repel them. They managed to secure their landing zone, then taking losses flooded the corridors and stormed the lava observatory room, which gave them access to an outside walkway. Despite battle droid snipers firing at them, the stormtroopers were able to capture the walkway.
Even with remnants of the feared [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]] striking at their ranks, the 501st Legion managed to take command over an overlook station from the droid forces. Then, the regrouped stormtroopers rushed into the factory control center, where they were able to destroy both of the droid prototype schematics of a new, unfinished battle droid prototype, ruining Dellso's plans of gaining the upper hand in possible future battles.<ref name="BFII" />

===Ending the battle===
{{Quote|Truth be told, we'd all had our fill of those gear-heads during the war, and we couldn't stomach the thought of going through another one. So we blew them up, blew up the droids, blew up the factory, damn near blew up the planet.|Retired clone trooper|<ref name="BFII" />}}
[[File:KleggerDestruction-BF2.jpg|thumb|right|225px|The orbital bombardment of the mining facility.]]
During the battle, a stormtrooper carrying an [[orbital strike beacon]] had been shot, and once retrieved, the beacon was set on the overlook to signal for the orbiting Star Destroyer to bombard the base. Imperial forces were tasked to recover the beacon. However, Gizor Dellso and his Geonosian bodyguards arrived to destroy the remaining stormtroopers, a wild firefight began. Though many stormtroopers were slain, Gizor Dellso's guards were killed, and he was then killed.<ref name="BFII" />

The troopers had just enough time to depart when their overhead forces executed an orbital bombardment command on the Mustafar complex, completely demolishing the mining facility and the droid factory.<ref name="BFII" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|The destruction of the mining facility put an end to any thoughts of a second "droid rebellion". For the first time in years the men of the 501st could relax and get about the business of maintaining an Empire rather than building one.|Retired clone trooper|<ref name="BFII" />}}
Following the battle, the 501st Legion shifted its focus to overseeing the Empire, not constructing one.<ref name="BFII" />

In the years following the battle and the destruction of the droid foundry, the [[Mensix Mining Facility/Legends|Mensix Mining Facility]] was built close to the [[Klegger Corp Mining Facility/Legends|Klegger Corp Mining Facility]] and was in operation during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="SWG">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Trials of Obi-Wan]]''</ref> This new facility would be the site of a [[Battle of Mustafar (Galactic Civil War)|battle]] between the [[Imperial Remnant]] and [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces.<ref name="BF:ES">''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The space battle and the surface battle are two campaign missions in ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''. The first mission, the space battle, is optional, like the other space levels in the game's campaign, whereas the battle on the planet's surface is required to advance to the next level.<ref name="BFII" />
